items.standfirst: Israel and Hezbollah have reached a Lebanon ceasefire agreement after their fighting delayed progression of the U.S.-Iran deal, a diplomat tells CBS News.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree ceasefire after intensified fighting threatens U.S.-Iran talks
items.standfirst: Israel and Lebanon’s Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire Friday after a deadly escalation between the two looked set to derail Washington — Tehran peace talks in Switzerland, a U.S. official tells NBC News
items.content: Israel and Lebanon’s Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire Friday after a deadly escalation between the two looked set to derail Washington — Tehran peace talks in Switzerland, a U.S. official tells NBC News

items.standfirst: Burnham hails ‘turning point’ for the country after resounding byelection victory over Reform UKPodcast: will Burnham become prime minister?What would ‘change’ look like if Burnham becomes PM?David Blunkett, the former Labour cabinet minister, has suggested that Keir Starmer should stand down after the Makerfield byelection.In an interview on the BBC’s Newsnight, Blunkett suggested that Starmer standing aside would be the best option for the party regardless of whether Andy Burnham wins tonight or loses. items.title: 'Political drama beckons' in the UK as Starmer vows to run in any leadership contest
items.standfirst: British Prime ​Minister Keir Starmer ​urged Labour members to unite ​after ‌the Makerfield ⁠by-election victory that returned ‌rival Andy Burnham to parliament, ⁠warning against infighting as pressure ​grows over the party ‌leadership. Burnham's election clears the way for his expectede bid to oust Starmer, who has grown increasingly unpopular. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot has more from London.
items.content: British Prime ​Minister Keir Starmer ​urged Labour members to unite ​after ‌the Makerfield ⁠by-election victory that returned ‌rival Andy Burnham to parliament, ⁠warning against infighting as pressure ​grows over the party ‌leadership. Burnham's election clears the way for his expectede bid to oust Starmer, who has grown increasingly unpopular. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot has more from London.

items.title: Labour hopes for 'smooth transition' as Starmer rival wins critical election
items.standfirst: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has congratulated Labour's Andi Burnham's win in the by-election that took place in the constituency of Makerfield on June 17, while saying he would fight any leadership challenge he is presented with. Burnham is Starmer's main rival, with many in Labour hoping that the new MP will now challenge the PM. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more.
items.content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has congratulated Labour's Andi Burnham's win in the by-election that took place in the constituency of Makerfield on June 17, while saying he would fight any leadership challenge he is presented with. Burnham is Starmer's main rival, with many in Labour hoping that the new MP will now challenge the PM. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more.

items.title: Burnham Wins in Makerfield - So What Happens Now?
items.standfirst: Burnham wins Makerfield by-election, paving way for leadership challenge against Starmer.
items.content: Burnham wins Makerfield by-election, paving way for leadership challenge against Starmer.

items.title: U.K. heads for leadership contest as Keir Starmer vows to fight after key rival secures vital win
items.standfirst: The mayor of Greater Manchester, who has vowed a leadership challenge to replace Starmer as prime minister, has come a step closer to power by securing a seat in Parliament.
items.content: The mayor of Greater Manchester, who has vowed a leadership challenge to replace Starmer as prime minister, has come a step closer to power by securing a seat in Parliament.

ai_summary: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield byelection

clusters.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers withdraw psychiatric defense for state case in CEO killing
clusters.neutral_headline: Luigi Mangione's lawyers withdraw psychiatric defense

items.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers withdraw psychiatric defense for state case in CEO killing
items.standfirst: Luigi Mangione's lawyers are withdrawing a psychiatric defense for his New York state murder trial. A judge had previously revealed Mangione, who has pleaded not guilty, would claim extreme emotional disturbance at the time he allegedly shot and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. CBS News legal contributor Caroline Polisi explains.
items.content: Luigi Mangione's lawyers are withdrawing a psychiatric defense for his New York state murder trial. A judge had previously revealed Mangione, who has pleaded not guilty, would claim extreme emotional disturbance at the time he allegedly shot and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. CBS News legal contributor Caroline Polisi explains.

items.title: Mangione's lawyers reverse course on psychiatric defence in state murder trial
items.standfirst: The defence team reverse course a day after saying they would argue he suffered "extreme emotional disturbance".
items.content: The defence team reverse course a day after saying they would argue he suffered "extreme emotional disturbance".

items.title: Luigi Mangione's legal team withdraws psychiatric defense
items.standfirst: Luigi Mangione's legal team says they withdrew a psychiatric defense​​ in his New York state murder trial one day after telling the court they would use it.
items.content: Luigi Mangione's legal team says they withdrew a psychiatric defense​​ in his New York state murder trial one day after telling the court they would use it.

clusters.title: Ghana brings reparations for transatlantic slave trade a step further
clusters.neutral_headline: Ghana brings reparations for transatlantic slave trade a step further

items.title: Ghana brings reparations for transatlantic slave trade a step further
items.standfirst: Ghana is taking the growing call for reparations over the transatlantic slave trade a significant step further towards concrete action. Weeks after the UN called it the 'gravest crime against humanity", leaders from around the continent and the Caribbean were in Accra for a conference on reparatory justice. Now, Ghana has adopted a 10-point plan to enforce the UN resolution. FRANCE 24's Justice Baidoo tells us more.
items.content: Ghana is taking the growing call for reparations over the transatlantic slave trade a significant step further towards concrete action. Weeks after the UN called it the 'gravest crime against humanity", leaders from around the continent and the Caribbean were in Accra for a conference on reparatory justice. Now, Ghana has adopted a 10-point plan to enforce the UN resolution. FRANCE 24's Justice Baidoo tells us more.

items.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
items.standfirst: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countriesBarbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement.Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...
items.content: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countriesBarbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement.Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

Plan aims to enforce UN resolution

items.standfirst: The national average for a gallon of regular gas in the U.S. drops to $3.99, the lowest since March 30.
items.content: The national average for a gallon of regular gas in the U.S. drops to $3.99, the lowest since March 30.

items.title: Gas prices fall below $4 for the first time since March
items.standfirst: Gas prices fell below $4 per gallon for the first time since March after more than three months of elevated prices as a result of the Iran war.
items.content: Gas prices fell below $4 per gallon for the first time since March after more than three months of elevated prices as a result of the Iran war.
